element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Early levels teach horizontal traversal without major hazards
Club-based enemy defeat (unique to series); stones can be ejected from club in up to 3 volleys
Bones used as currency in shops for spells and ability upgrades
Purchase spells and upgrades to extend Big Nose's combat/traversal abilities
Critical for survival; grants temporary invulnerability to single-hit deaths
Precision jumping required; death on contact with gaps
Big Nose cannot swim; instant death on contact
Final island combines all previous mechanics at peak difficulty

typical_tension_triggers: Encounters with lava/water near traversal paths
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Long campaign with escalating difficulty across four island worlds; single-hit death mechanic (without stones) creates sustained tension; relief provided by economic progression (bone collection/shop upgrades) rather than frequent safe zones

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Bones scattered in explorable areas encourage non-linear collection routes; shop locations may signal areas of relative safety

difficulty_curve.early_game: Paradise Island introduces club combat, bone economy, and shop system in relatively safe environments; foundational mechanics with low environmental hazard density
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Monster Island escalates with pitfalls, lava, and water hazards; platforming precision becomes critical; players must manage stone power-up scarcity
difficulty_curve.late_game: Terror Island and Chaos Island combine trap mechanics with earlier hazards; sustained difficulty spike; limited opportunity to recover from mistakes due to single-hit death model
notable_spikes: Transition from Paradise Island to Monster Island (introduction of platforming hazards over combat focus)
Terror Island entry (mechanical traps layer atop environmental threats)
Chaos Island (final island combines all mechanics; significant spike in enemy density and trap complexity)

notable_level_patterns.description: Four distinct islands (Paradise, Monster, Terror, Chaos) serve as difficulty tiers and thematic zones; each island contains multiple levels with escalating local difficulty
Bone collection gates access to shop upgrades; ability upgrades and spells must be purchased with collected bones, creating a progression loop separate from level completion
Big Nose dies in one hit unless protected by stone power-ups; creates risk/reward tension around stone pickup timing and conservation

sources_used: Wikipedia: Big Nose the Caveman (redirects Big Nose Freaks Out) - gameplay section describing club mechanics, bone economy, shop system, hazards, and difficulty
